name: Intent fit
description: The screen should support the user job implied by the selected navigation item or workflow.
example_good: Run Timeline shows ordered phases for the selected audit run.
example_bad: Run Timeline shows a generic latest-report card without timeline content.

name: Context clarity
description: The screen identifies the current object being inspected, such as project, report, run, timestamp, and status.
example_good: The page says Latest run, Workspace Control, generated timestamp, and passed status.
example_bad: The page lists phases without saying which run or report produced them.

name: Workflow continuity
description: Multi-step workflows show what happened before, where the user is now, and what to inspect next.
example_good: Scenario detail shows each step, status, screenshot, and failed assertion.
example_bad: The screen only shows a final state with no preceding actions.

name: Evidence proximity
description: Claims in QA/reporting tools should be close to screenshots, traces, issues, or fix packets that support them.
example_good: A failed scenario step includes its screenshot and evidence text.
example_bad: The page states a scenario failed but the screenshot is only available elsewhere with no context.

name: Information hierarchy
description: Primary content is prominent and raw/debug detail stays behind tabs, details, or explicit debug views.
example_good: Summary cards show counts first, with Raw JSON behind a dedicated nav item.
example_bad: Users must read raw JSON to understand whether a run passed.

name: Navigation promise
description: A navigation item should deliver content that matches its label and make that content identity clear.
example_good: Screenshots opens an evidence gallery labelled Screenshots.
example_bad: Run Timeline opens a generic report page that never mentions timeline or phases.

name: State and empty-state honesty
description: When content is empty or unavailable, explain why and what creates or loads it.
example_good: No issues found explains the checks that ran and links to scenarios/screenshots.
example_bad: No data is shown without explaining whether the app has not been audited yet.

name: Actionability
description: The screen offers the next appropriate action such as open, copy, verify, generate, or inspect.
example_good: A fix packet exposes Copy prompt and verification command.
example_bad: A repair issue is shown without any way to copy or inspect the fix packet.

name: Visual comprehension
description: Related information is grouped, scan-friendly, and long paths/data are controlled.
example_good: Long paths truncate in list cards but remain available via title or detail.
example_bad: A local path overflows its card and hides adjacent controls.

name: Safety clarity
description: Tools that can run agents or commands clearly distinguish manual, mock, Codex, and destructive behavior.
example_good: Settings says Codex is manual/unconfigured and no secrets are shown.
example_bad: An Apply Fix button appears without explaining whether it will execute an agent.
